title: Spot Ocean Cluster
description: An Ocean cluster is a serverless Kubernetes infrastructure engine that automatically manages and optimizes cloud infrastructure for containers, handling node provisioning, scaling, and cost optimization.

id.description: The unique identifier of the Ocean cluster.

name.description: The name of the Ocean cluster.

controllerClusterId.description: The Ocean controller cluster identifier used by the controller pod.

region.description: The cloud provider region where the cluster is deployed.

autoScaler.description: The autoscaler configuration for the Ocean cluster.

cooldown.description: Cooldown period in seconds between scaling actions.

maxMemoryGib.description: Maximum memory in GiB for the cluster.

capacity.description: The capacity configuration for the Ocean cluster.

strategy.description: The optimization strategy for the Ocean cluster.

spotPercentage.description: Percentage of spot instances in the cluster.

fallbackToOd.description: Whether to fall back to on-demand when spot is unavailable.

utilizeCommitments.description: Whether to utilize savings plans and commitments.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the cluster was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the cluster was last updated.
